text: In addition to revocation or suspension of a registration pursuant to any other provision of this chapter, all registrations issued to a registrant pursuant to this article may be revoked where the holder is convicted of a second or subsequent violation committed within a period of twelve months of subdivision three of § 2262 (Limitations on registrations) . Where a revocation is issued pursuant to this section, no new limited use vehicle registration shall be issued to such person for a period of one year from the date of such revocation.
markdown:
# Vehicle & Traffic Law
## Title 11
#### Article 48
##### § 2263 — Revocation and suspension

In addition to revocation or suspension of a registration pursuant to any other provision of this chapter, all registrations issued to a registrant pursuant to this article may be revoked where the holder is convicted of a second or subsequent violation committed within a period of twelve months of subdivision three of § 2262 (Limitations on registrations) . Where a revocation is issued pursuant to this section, no new limited use vehicle registration shall be issued to such person for a period of one year from the date of such revocation.

*Last modified: Sep. 22, 2014*

Where a revocation is issued pursuant to this section, no new limited use vehicle registration shall be issued to such person for a period of one year from the date of such revocation.
